description: Tabbed panels. TabsList is role="tablist"; each TabsTrigger is role="tab" with aria-selected and aria-controls pointing at its TabsContent (role="tabpanel", labelled by the trigger). Radix implements the WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with roving tabindex and optional automatic activation.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the active tab trigger, then into its panel, then out.
Moves focus between triggers in a horizontal tablist (and activates the tab if activationMode is "automatic", the default).
Same as Left / Right when orientation="vertical".
Moves focus to the first / last trigger.
Activates the focused trigger when activationMode is "manual".

focus.notes: Only the active trigger is in the tab sequence (roving tabindex); other triggers are tabindex=-1 but reachable via arrow keys. Both TabsTrigger and TabsContent have focus-visible:ring-2 rings.

announcements: Tablist announces as a tab list with its count.
Each trigger announces as a tab with its selected state.
Switching to a new tab moves the "selected" state and (if activationMode="automatic") shows the new panel immediately.
screenReader.notes: For panels with heavy content, set activationMode="manual" so arrow keys move focus without switching panels; the user presses Enter / Space to commit. This avoids loading / rendering delays as users scan.

contrast.text: Active trigger uses text-foreground on bg-background; inactive uses text-muted-foreground. Both meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Active trigger gains a shadow; focus ring uses ring-ring. Meets AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Only a background transition on active state; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Keep trigger labels concise; they're read in a list and long labels get truncated visually.
Consider activationMode="manual" for tabs that load expensive content, so arrow-key browsing doesn't thrash.
Don't nest Tabs more than one level; nested tabs are confusing for keyboard and screen reader users alike.
